problem.title: Merge Sorted Array
problem.description: You are given two integer arrays nums1 and nums2, sorted in non-decreasing order, and two integers m and n, representing the number of elements in nums1 and nums2 respectively.
assumptions: nums1 has enough space (m+n) to hold all elements.

example.explanation: Merge nums2 into nums1 in-place. Start from the back to avoid overwriting.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Pointers from End [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Use three pointers starting from the ends of both arrays and the end of the allocated space in nums1, filling in values from largest to smallest. Working backwards avoids overwriting elements in nums1 that haven't been processed yet. The key insight is that the tail of nums1 is always empty buffer space, so we can safely write there without a temporary array.
